summary: High‑energy early ’78 show with scorching Scarlet>Fire and Samson, captured on a mostly solid soundboard recording despite some mic hiccups.
review: Reviewers rave about the second set – a blistering Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain, an epic Samson & Delilah, ripping Truckin' and a soaring The Other One – all driven by Jerry’s raging guitar.  The soundboard source is generally clear, though a few note dead or weak mics (Bobby’s early mic, Samson’s feed) and occasional bass loss.  Overall the performance outweighs the minor technical flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently five‑star praise for musical intensity and only modest, isolated recording issues; strong consensus across eight reviews.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp overall mix with occasional mic failures (Bobby's early mic dead, Samson & Delilah feed problem) and some tracks lacking bass or ambience; no major drop‑outs.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set deemed average, but the second set is universally hailed as a highlight – intense jams, seamless transitions and a powerful encore that caps an unforgettable night.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Raving about his ‘raging’ solos, snarling tone on Bertha, blistering Scarlet>Fire jam and screaming U.S. Blues finale.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass intro to The Other One praised for saving the drum section; solid throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Early mic failure noted, but Bobby’s joke described as a mini‑Zappa moment; overall presence felt despite technical glitch.
band_member_comments.Keys: Extra weaving with Keith at the end of Wharf Rat mentioned positively; no major critiques.
band_member_comments.Drums: Random thumping in the drum break noted; otherwise the drum segment is typical for the era.
